title: Tarr Seirr
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Tarr}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:Tarr_seirr.jpg]] |name=Tarr Seirr |homeworld=[[Cerea/Legends|Cerea]]{{DB|character|tarrseirr|Seirr, Tarr}} |birth= |death=[[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] (13:9), [[Hypori/Legends|Hypori]]{{CW|20}} |species=[[Cerean/Legends|Cerean]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height=1.9 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes=Grayish blue |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **[[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Why have they stopped?''"
"''Master?''"
"''Go! Go!|Ki-Adi-Mundi and Tarr Seirr during the Battle of Hypori|}} '''Tarr Seirr''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Cerean/Legends|Cerean]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who fought in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] against the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]]. Raised by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], he became a [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|Jedi Watchman]] for his home [[Cerean system|system Cerea]], and helped advise the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] government during the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]]. When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] broke out, he was transferred from [[Cerea/Legends|Cerea]] to fight alongside his long-time idol, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]]. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], he was selected to be a part of the elite task force sent to [[Battle of Hypori/Legends|destroy]] a [[Hypori droid factory|droid factory]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] in planet [[Hypori/Legends|Hypori]]. However, the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], which carried the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and their [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]], were blasted out of the sky before they could even land. Only Seirr and five other Jedi managed to survive the crash landing, but they were quickly surrounded by a [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|droid army]] under the command of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]. After the remaining Jedi took a shelter from one of the crushed [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|assault ships]], Grievous ordered the droid forces to stop, and personally entered [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|hand-to-hand combat]] with the trapped Jedi warriors. During the fight, Seirr tried to strike Grievous from behind, but he was grabbed by the [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] general's clawed foot and crushed to [[Death/Legends|death]], then thrown into the wall of the ruined assault ship along with [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]]. ==Biography== ===Early life=== Born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Cerea/Legends|Cerea]] and raised by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], the [[Cerean/Legends|Cerean]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Tarr Seirr always idolized [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]], a Cerean [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] and a member of the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] who served as the [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|Watchman]] for the [[Cerean Quadrant]] for decades. After passing his [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Trials of Knighthood]], Seirr was [[Knighting Ceremony/Legends|elevated]] to the rank of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]. During the growing [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], members of the Council found themselves too occupied, and Mundi relinquished his position as Watchman to Seirr. Shortly before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], when Cerean government indicated the planet's uncertainty to officially side with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], Seirr advised the Cerean government to join the Republic to prevent the planet from falling to the Separatists of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. However, Cerea ultimately chose neutrality during the war.''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]'' ===Attack on Hypori=== {{Quote|Our predicament is dire, but do not despair. Focus. We are ''Jedi''!|Ki-Adi-Mundi, to the survived Jedi during the Battle of Hypori|audio=OurPredicamentIsDire-CW20.flac|}} [[File:Tarr_seirr_hypori.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Tarr Seirr while searching for Grievous.]] When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] broke out in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Watchmen were scattered to different battlefront locations, and Seirr was reassigned from Cerea to a strategic battlefront location. He was secretly reluctant to depart from his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], as the planet was left susceptible to danger of the Separatist [[Battle of Cerea|invasion]], but nevertheless obeyed the order and left Cerea. As the Clone Wars raged on, Seirr had a chance to fight alongside his role model, [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]]. Around 22 BBY, Jedi General [[Daakman Barrek]] uncovered a [[Hypori droid factory|massive CIS droid factory]] on planet [[Hypori/Legends|Hypori]].{{DB|character|daakmanbarrek|Barrek, Daakman}} An elite task force, consisting of Barrek and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Sha'a Gi]], along with Seirr, and Jedi Generals Ki-Adi-Mundi, [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]], [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]] and [[K'Kruhk]] were dispatched to the planet to [[Battle of Hypori/Legends|destroy]] the factory, reinforced by an army of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]]. However, the mission turned out to be a disaster; as the Republic [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|ships]] emerged from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], Confederacy's [[Space mine/Legends|orbital mines]] caused the majority of the transports to crash to the planet, and straight behind the Separatist's lines. Seirr and the other Jedi survived the landing, but nearly all clone troopers were [[Death/Legends|killed]]. The situation rapidly grew worse when the survived troopers were surrounded by a massive droid army that was stationed on the planet under the command of the [[Supreme Commander of the Droid Armies/Legends|Supreme Commander of the Droid Armies]], General [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]. During the ensuing fight, Seirr received a wound on his left [[Limb/Legends|arm]], Barrek was killed by Grievous, and all remaining clone troopers were shot by [[Droid/Legends|droid]] forces. Separated from other survivors and chased by [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droids]] and [[OG-9 homing spider droid/Legends|OG-9 homing spider droids]], Seirr and Ki-Adi-Mundi tried to retreat on one of the crashed [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]]. Grievous, who wanted to slay the Jedi personally, ordered the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|droid army]] to halt, while Seirr and Mundi ran inside the wrecked [[Starship/Legends|ship]], where they reunited with the other Jedi. Moments later, Grievous told the survivors to prepare to meet a "warrior's death", and started to move toward the wreckage. ===Final duel=== {{Quote|It's close, but where?|Tarr Seirr referring to Grievous' footsteps|}} [[File:TarrGrievous-CW20.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Tarr Seirr is killed by Grievous.]] Inside the ruined ship, the Jedi activated their [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]], preparing to face off against the [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] general. However, scared by the approaching threat, Padawan Sha'a Gi mentally broke, and rushed out of the ruins. Grievous quickly crushed him on earth, killing him, and hid inside the ship. The remaining Jedi backed off in the center of the cruiser, but were suddenly attacked by the reemerging Grievous, who leaped from shadows in the middle of the Jedi. Although the [[Jedi hunter/Legends|Jedi hunter]] was surrounded, he managed to hold his own during the [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|duel]]. Grievous soon gained the upper hand, and after striking Seirr and the rest of the Jedi away, he severely wounded K'Kruhk, who fell unconscious. Shaak Ti then used [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]] to throw scrap metal against Grievous, while Seirr tried to defeat the general by striking him in the back. However, Grievous blocked the metal with his lightsabers, and after noticing Seirr, grabbed the Jedi Knight's head with his [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] foot, and crushed Seirr's head against the ground, killing him. He then threw the lifeless body of Seirr against the roof along with injured Aayla Secura. Only Ki-Adi-Mundi and a severely wounded Shaak Ti, Secura, and K'Kruhk survived the engagement. Later on, Seirr's fears transpired to be reality when unprotected Cerea became a battleground as Republic and Separatist forces fought to claim its allegiance. As a result, much of the serene wilderness of the planet was despoiled in the fighting, and many Cereans were killed. ==Personality and traits== [[File:SeirrLightsaber.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Tarr Seirr with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].]] {{Quote|I can't see anything.|Seirr, while hearing Grievous' footsteps|}} Raised by the Jedi Order, Tarr Seirr strongly believed in the Jedi cause. He respected the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]], even when it meant to leave something important behind; when he was assigned to a more tactical location from his homeworld Cerea, he hid his reluctance of leaving the planet to a danger and obeyed the assign. He was very fond for Cerea, and didn't want it to join the Separatist cause. Seirr greatly revered Jedi Master Ki-Adi-Mundi, and when he relinquished his position as Watchman to Seirr, the young Cerean took the role very seriously, and helped advise the ruling Cerean government during the Separatist threat. During the Battle of Hypori, Seirr asked Mundi's advice during their retreating, and stayed close to the Jedi Master even inside the shelter; when Mundi tried to stop Padawan Sha'a Gi for bolting, Seirr was the first Jedi who followed the Cerean Jedi Master. Seirr was seemingly nervous when General Grievous approached, but didn't later hesitate to attack the cyborg. ==Powers and abilities== A Jedi Knight, Seirr was an accomplished swordsman, his skill with a blade rivaling many Jedi Masters. Favoring Form 4, Ataru, Seirr proved to be an adept duelist, and because of this, as well as his Cerean heritage, he was selected to succeed Ki Adi Mundi as the Jedi Watchman of the Cerea System. Even with the combined strength of him and the four Jedi generals, however, he was unable to defeat Grievous, who blocked and dodged the lightsaber strikes of the Jedi and ultimately killed him. ==Behind the scenes== Tarr Seirr was a character created for ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'', where he was voiced by [[Daran Norris]]. In the episode [[Chapter 20 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|20]], he was only credited as '''Warrior #3'''. Much of his backstory was later written on the official [[Star Wars]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]]. He also appeared in a mobile strategy game ''[[Star Wars: Battle for the Republic]]''.''[[Star Wars: Battle for the Republic]]'' ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Tarr Seirr}} *{{CW|20}} {{1st}} *''[[Star Wars: Battle for the Republic]]'' *''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]'' {{Mo}} {{C|As Tarr Seir}} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|86|Unknown Soldier: The Story of General Grievous}} *{{DB|character|tarrseirr|Seirr, Tarr}} {{1stID|Tarr Seirr}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |cs=Tarr Seirr |de=Tarr Seirr |es=Tarr Seirr |fr=Tarr Seirr |it=Tarr Seirr |hu=Tarr Seirr |nl=Tarr Seirr |pt=Tarr Seirr |fi=Tarr Seirr |deGA=1 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Seirr, Tarr}} [[Category:Cereans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Jedi Watchmen]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Post-Ruusan Jedi Knights]]
